description: Fix high-severity inaccuracies in the troubleshooting and configuration reference pages. The troubleshooting page points to the wrong file for the auth secret, omits a required CLI flag, and references a non-existent flag. The configuration page is missing the `port` config field.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix secret location and CLI flag errors in troubleshooting.mdx
userStories.description: As a user debugging authentication issues, I want the troubleshooting page to point to the correct secret file and use valid CLI flags so that I can resolve issues without confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: The troubleshooting page references `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json` (not `~/.opentabs/config.json`) as the location of the auth secret
The command to retrieve the secret uses `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret` (includes `--show-secret` flag)
The `opentabs logs --no-follow` reference is replaced with `opentabs logs` (the default behavior without `-f` already shows recent logs and exits)
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `docs/content/docs/reference/troubleshooting.mdx`.

1. Around line 104: Change `~/.opentabs/config.json` to `~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json`. The secret is stored in auth.json as `{ "secret": "<hex>" }`, NOT in config.json.
2. Around lines 106-107: The command `opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret` returns a masked secret. Add the `--show-secret` flag: `opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret`. See `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` line 54 for the masking logic.
3. Around line 122: `opentabs logs --no-follow` uses a flag that doesn't exist. The `opentabs logs` command supports `--lines <n>`, `-f/--follow`, and `--plugin <name>` â€” no `--no-follow`. The default behavior (without `-f`) already shows recent logs and exits. Replace with just `opentabs logs`.

userStories.title: Add missing port config field to configuration.mdx
userStories.description: As a user configuring the MCP server, I want the port field documented in the configuration reference so that I understand how port configuration works.
acceptanceCriteria: The Config Fields table in configuration.mdx includes a `port` row with type `number`, default `9515`, and a description explaining it sets the MCP server port
The field description notes that `opentabs config set port <n>` writes this value and `opentabs start` reads it
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Edit `docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx`. Add a `port` entry to the Config Fields table. The table currently lists: `localPlugins`, `tools`, `browserToolPolicy`, `permissions`, `skipConfirmation`. Add `port` with:
- Type: `number`
- Default: `9515` (from `platform/shared/src/constants.ts` line 16: `DEFAULT_PORT = 9515`)
- Description: MCP server port. Set via `opentabs config set port <n>` or the `--port` CLI flag. The CLI resolves port from (highest precedence first): `--port` flag > `OPENTABS_PORT` env var > `config.json port` > default 9515.

Note: The `port` field is written by `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` line 347 and read by `platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts` for the server process.
